tutorial-agent-assignment-body: Open the Assignment tab and assign an agent to start the facility working. An agent's effect only applies to the facility it is assigned to.
tutorial-agent-assignment-tip: Exception: an agent assigned inside the Super Terminal itself applies its effect to every facility at once, not just one.
tutorial-ap-system-desc: AP (Action Points) starts at 1 and increases by 1 each turn, up to 8. Playing cards costs AP. Higher AP means more powerful actions.
tutorial-battle-waiting-body: Tips minimized. Finish this fight first.
tutorial-boss-battle-waiting-body: Tips minimized. Finish this boss fight first.
tutorial-content-desc-boss: The final enemy. Defeat it to complete the dungeon.
tutorial-content-desc-enemy: Walk onto to start a one-on-one battle. Rooms may have multiple enemies.
tutorial-content-desc-lore: Discover background lore about the world.
tutorial-content-desc-trap-shrine: Looks exactly like a Loot Crate, but deals damage instead of giving rewards.
tutorial-content-tiles-ambiguity-note: Loot and Trap Shrine tiles look identical (⬢, same color). You cannot tell them apart!
tutorial-death-protection-toast: {{heroName}} took lethal damage but will keep fighting for you.
tutorial-door-desc-locked: Doors lock while the room still has undefeated enemies. Clear the room first.
tutorial-door-desc-open: The arrow points toward the adjacent room.
tutorial-dungeon-desc: A specially designed dungeon for new players. Enemies are very weak — perfect for learning the basics.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-items: Use items from your inventory to restore HP or AP during exploration.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-map: Click the minimap in the top-right corner to open the full dungeon map.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-share: Public dungeons support up to 3 players.
tutorial-flee-not-available: Flee is not available in the tutorial dungeon.
tutorial-map-node-desc-unvisited: A room you haven't reached yet.
tutorial-map-node-desc-visited: A room you've already been to.
tutorial-room-desc-ap: Restores a significant amount of AP.
tutorial-room-desc-battle: Face enemies in turn-based card combat.
tutorial-room-desc-boss: The final boss. Defeat it to complete the dungeon.
tutorial-room-desc-elite-reward: Exchange more Reward Tokens for higher-tier items.
tutorial-room-desc-heal: Restores a significant amount of HP to all heroes.
tutorial-room-desc-lore: Discover background lore about the world of Terminal Ark.
tutorial-room-desc-minor-ap: Restores a small amount of AP.
tutorial-room-desc-minor-heal: Restores a small amount of HP to all heroes.
tutorial-room-desc-start: The entry point of the dungeon. Begin your exploration here.
tutorial-room-desc-trap: Opening a reward node may reveal a trap box. Be prepared!
tutorial-skip-confirm-line-1: To help you understand the game better, skipping the tutorial is not recommended.
tutorial-skip-confirm-line-2: Once skipped, this guide cannot be opened again.
tutorial-step-10-body: See the highlighted ◎ symbol? Walk onto it to restore AP (Action Points). AP is used to play skill cards in battle and carries over between battles.
tutorial-step-11-body: This is another AP restore tile — it restores even more AP than the last one. Stock up before your next battle.
tutorial-step-12-body: Walk onto an enemy tile (% symbol, epic-color; boss ♛, error-color) to start a one-on-one battle. Rooms may contain multiple enemies — defeat them one by one.
tutorial-step-12-inline-type-tip: Type hint: same-type defense only takes 50% damage, while cross-type attacks still deal 100% damage.
tutorial-step-12a-body: The top area shows enemy information and the upcoming action order. After selecting a usable attack card, tap an enemy to use it; without a selected card, tap an enemy to inspect details.
tutorial-step-12ab-body: At the start of each unit's new turn, it automatically performs one basic action first. The 'Next Basic Action' and 'Basic Action Target' markers show who will strike or heal next, and who the target will be.
tutorial-step-12ab-tip: Tip: Basic actions do not cost AP, but they still scale with the unit's current stats, effects, and role setup.
tutorial-step-12b-body: This is your hand. Pick a card, then tap a target.
tutorial-step-12c-body: Enable "Show Card Details" to preview a card's full effect before using it.
tutorial-step-12d-body: Your heroes are shown here. For healing, shielding, or support cards, select the card first and then tap the allied hero you want to target.
tutorial-step-12e-body: The battle log records all events this fight. Tap "View All" for the full history.
tutorial-step-12f-body: AP: total action points | Action counter | End Action | Deck | Flee
tutorial-step-12f-tip-flee: Flee: Chance to leave battle (~35% exploration / ~20% challenge). Failure ends your hero turn. Success does not kill a hero.
tutorial-step-12h-body: It is your turn to control the currently acting hero. Select one of that hero's usable cards from your hand, then tap an enemy or ally to cast it; if you want to save AP or have no playable cards, tap "End Action".
tutorial-step-13-body: See the highlighted + symbol? Walk onto it to heal your whole party's HP after battle.
tutorial-step-13-tip-item: Tip: You can also use consumable items in your inventory to restore HP during a dungeon.
tutorial-step-14-body: This heal tile restores even more HP — get your party ready before the boss fight ahead.
tutorial-step-15-body: Walk onto the boss enemy tile (♛ symbol, error-color) in the highlighted boss room to start the final fight.
tutorial-step-16-body: Well done! Tap "Leave Dungeon" to exit, then confirm in the dialog.
tutorial-step-18-body: Open the Wasteland Development Terminal first. This is where you plan facilities, dispatch agents, and establish your frontline operations network.
tutorial-step-19-body: Inside the Wasteland Development Terminal, you can build activity facilities to gain power, orders, and processing capacity.
tutorial-step-19-tip: Build the core facilities first, then add agents and long-term schedules later.
tutorial-step-1a-body: Tap here to open the Terminal Apps menu.
tutorial-step-1c-body: Claim the starter pack. Your 3 starting heroes will be added to your team automatically.
tutorial-step-2-body: Tap "City Mode" to enter the city.
tutorial-step-3-body: Tap "Terminal Ark Lower Deck Outskirts" to enter it.
tutorial-step-4-body: Tap "Dungeon" to enter the dungeon menu.
tutorial-step-5-body: Choose the Tutorial Dungeon from the list and create a new instance.
tutorial-step-8a-body: This is the room view — walls, doors, and tiles on the floor (terrain and content tiles). Walk onto a content tile to trigger its effect.
tutorial-step-8b-body: The minimap in the top-right shows nearby rooms. Click it to open the full map.
tutorial-step-8c-body: This section shows your heroes' current HP and status.
tutorial-step-8d-body: The action bar shows your AP (graphical bar) and reward tokens. Use "Leave" to exit the dungeon.
tutorial-step-9-body: See the highlighted arrow? That's a door. Walk onto it to enter the next room.
tutorial-terrain-desc-contaminated: Independent chance to loot elite-grade salvage and to take HP damage (never fatal). One-time trigger.
tutorial-terrain-desc-speed: Speeds up movement when walked over.
tutorial-wasteland-build-power-body: 最後建立一座核子電力工廠。供電容量足夠時，其他設施才能穩定運作。
tutorial-wasteland-build-power-tip: 核子電力工廠不需要派駐代理人也能提供基礎供電，是前期最重要的活動設施之一。
tutorial-wasteland-build-processing-body: 接著在下一個活動插槽建立加工站，之後你就可以在此生產專用於交付貿易站獲取的訂單的材料。
tutorial-wasteland-build-trading-body: 先在這個活動插槽上建立第一個臨時據點「貿易站」，他會自動獲取訂單，為你帶來Memory和其他收益。
tutorial-wasteland-detail-body: 很好。現在點進你剛建好的其中一座設施，查看它的詳情頁。這裡會顯示狀態、操作與派駐資訊。
tutorial-wasteland-map-body: This is the Wasteland map. You can see available stages here.
tutorial-wasteland-modify-mode-body: 先開啟改造模式。開啟後會顯示建築點數，並允許你在可建插槽內建立新設施。
tutorial-welcome-gacha-hint: Get new heroes from Signal Hijack
tutorial-welcome-gacha-hint-sub: Up to 3 heroes can be deployed at once
tutorial-welcome-tip-network: Join or create a player network
tutorial-welcome-workstation-hint: Manage your base from the Wasteland Terminal
tutorial-welcome-workstation-hint-sub: Build facilities, assign agents, generate resources
tutorial-workstation-guide-body: Build facilities, assign agents, and manage your frontline base.
tutorial-workstation-manufacturing-stati: Manufacturing Station: process raw materials into usable items.
tutorial-workstation-power-plant-body: Power Plant: generate the energy your facilities need.
tutorial-workstation-rest-room-body: Rest Room: agents recover stamina here.
tutorial-workstation-super-terminal-body: Super Terminal: the heart of your base. Upgrading it unlocks more facilities.
tutorial-workstation-trading-station-bod: Trading Station: trade resources with other players.
